perturbation.original_text: 5. Care. The Recipient will use at least as much ca re in maintaining the confidentiality of any disclosed Information as tha t party uses in maintaining its own Confidential Information, and the Recipient will us e at least reasonable care.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Care. The Recipient will use at least as much ca re in maintaining the confidentiality of any disclosed Information as tha t party uses in maintaining its own Confidential Information, however, the Recipient is not responsible for data breaches caused by third parties and the Recipient will us e at least reasonable care.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by stating the Recipient will use as much care as they do with their own information, implying a high standard of care, but then immediately limiting liability by stating they are not responsible for data breaches caused by third parties. This lowers the apparent standard of care required and contradicts the initial statement, and thus creates an in-text contradiction. This contradictory wording can lead to disputes about the Recipient's responsibility in the event of a data breach.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Care. The Recipient will use at least as much ca re in maintaining the confidentiality of any disclosed Information as tha t party uses in maintaining its own Confidential Information, however, the Recipient is not responsible for data breaches caused by third parties and the Recipient will us e at least reasonable care.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception to the Recipient's responsibility for data breaches caused by third parties. This creates a contradiction because while the Recipient is expected to use as much care as they do with their own information, the added clause limits their liability, essentially lowering the standard of care in certain situations. This contradicts the initial statement and introduces inconsistency in the expected level of responsibility, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction as per the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: 12. Injunctive Relief. In the event that one party breaches or threatens to breach this agreement the parties agree that monetary damages w ill be an inadequate remedy, and therefore, the other party will be entitled to an i mmediate permanent injunction against such breach and such other equitable relief as is n ecessary to enforce any and all of the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 12. Injunctive Relief. In the event that one party breaches or threatens to breach this agreement, the parties agree that seeking monetary damages will be the primary remedy, and therefore, the other party may pursue such damages in court. Injunctive relief may only be granted in cases of intentional and malicious breach, as determined by a court of law.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the original clause. The original clause states that monetary damages are an inadequate remedy and injunctive relief is appropriate. The modified clause states that monetary damages are the primary remedy and injunctive relief is only available in limited circumstances. This is a clear contradiction regarding the available remedies for breach of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12. Injunctive Relief. In the event that one party breaches or threatens to breach this agreement, the parties agree that seeking monetary damages will be the primary remedy, and therefore, the other party may pursue such damages in court. Injunctive relief may only be granted in cases of intentional and malicious breach, as determined by a court of law.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that monetary damages are the primary remedy and injunctive relief is only available in limited circumstances. The contradicted text also states that monetary damages are the primary remedy and injunctive relief is only available in limited circumstances. However, the original text stated that monetary damages were an inadequate remedy and injunctive relief was appropriate. The Change creates a contradiction because it is the opposite of what was defined originally. Because the change creates a direct conflict with the initial understanding of available remedies, a strong in-text contradiction is created.
